message:
feat: async dispatch, channel-free SSI ledger, robust ALTER TABLE schema fidelity, and partial-failure benchmark accounting

AST:
- Add SQL keyword quoting to identifier display (needs_quoting, write_ident,
  write_qualified_name) so reserved words round-trip correctly through
  parse -> display -> re-parse
- Emit WITHOUT ROWID and STRICT as comma-separated table options matching
  SQLite grammar
- QualifiedName Display delegates to write_qualified_name for consistent
  quoting

Core (commit_repair):
- Replace Condvar-based TwoPhaseQueue with asupersync Semaphore + mpsc
  channel, eliminating spurious wake-ups and ticket bookkeeping
- Introduce ReceiverOrderState for strict reservation-sequence delivery
- Add ThreadParkWaker + block_on_future_with_timeout for bridging async
  semaphore acquire into synchronous call sites
- SendPermit now borrows the shared state (lifetime-scoped) and carries
  both a logical permit and a signal permit

Core (compat_persist):
- build_create_table_sql now renders UNIQUE/PRIMARY KEY/FOREIGN KEY/CHECK
  table constraints, WITHOUT ROWID, embedded-quote identifiers, and
  typeless columns faithfully
- Extract PK, FK, UNIQUE, and CHECK constraints from sqlite_master SQL
  during load_from_sqlite and populate TableSchema fields
- Hydrate unique-column-group enforcement from parsed indexes
- Refactor rowid-alias payload detection into hydrate_rowid_alias_value

Core (connection):
- TriggerFrame gains rowid_alias_col_idx for hidden-rowid resolution in
  OLD/NEW pseudo-columns
- Multi-row INSERT VALUES with triggers or FK enforcement is replayed
  row-by-row to ensure per-row trigger firing and constraint checks
- INSERT ... SELECT fallback preserves explicit column lists and uses
  resolve_insert_target_layout (replaces resolve_insert_select_target_layout)
- DML LIMIT/ORDER BY key selection builds AST-based SELECT instead of
  string interpolation, fixing quoting for reserved-word identifiers
- coerce_explicit_rowid_value replaces inline match for TEXT/REAL rowid
  coercion

E2E (concurrency_showcase, lib, perf_runner, report_render):
- Force PRAGMA busy_timeout=0 in benchmark harness configs so retry/abort
  counters reflect harness-level contention, not SQLite internal sleeping
- CellOutcome gains is_fully_comparable() and comparable_summary() to
  exclude partial-measurement-failure cells from engine comparisons
- benchmark_summary_error detects partial and total iteration failures
- Narrative text updated: "retryable batch restarts" replaces
  "conflict-related aborts" / "first-committer-wins retry"
- Speedup denominator fix: divide by fsqlite median (not sqlite3)
- New tests for partial-failure exclusion, busy-timeout=0 rendering,
  zero-denominator speedup, scaling analysis with error cells

Harness (tests):
- Morsel dispatcher and vectorized benchmark tests migrate from
  execute_with_barriers to execute_with_barriers_on_runtime with explicit
  Runtime construction
- commit_pipeline_two_phase adds out-of-order send/receive ordering test
  and fixes capacity-blocking test to clone sender per thread

MVCC (ssi_abort_policy):
- SsiEvidenceLedger replaces background thread + mpsc::channel with
  inline pending queue + try_flush_pending / flush_pending
- Eliminates await_quiescence polling; readers flush synchronously
- with_locked replaces with_locked_state, now generic over any Mutex<T>
- New test: record_async buffers drafts while state lock is held, then
  flushes in order on snapshot

Parser:
- Round-trip test for CREATE TABLE ... WITHOUT ROWID, STRICT
- Round-trip test for trigger on reserved-word table name ("order")

VDBE (codegen):
- TableSchema gains without_rowid and primary_key_constraints fields
- emit_upsert_expr / emit_upsert_assignments thread hidden-rowid
  register through all recursive expression arms for correct
  excluded.rowid / rowid resolution in DO UPDATE SET
- emit_default_value validates self-containedness via
  default_expr_is_self_contained (rejects column refs, subqueries,
  placeholders)
- parse_default_expr simplified; validation moved to caller
- New integration tests for expression defaults, non-constant default
  rejection, and hidden-rowid upsert resolution

VDBE (vectorized_dispatch):
- execute_with_barriers and execute_single_pipeline become async,
  using Runtime::current_handle + spawn instead of std::thread::spawn
- Workers wrapped in catch_unwind with pipeline-level Cx cancellation
  on panic
- New execute_with_barriers_on_runtime sync wrapper for callers that
  own the runtime
- dispatch_checkpoint gates each morsel iteration on Cx cancellation
- Morsel capacity pre-calculation moved after config validation
- DispatchError gains RuntimeUnavailable and Cancelled variants

VDBE (bench):
- vectorized_dispatch benchmark uses RuntimeBuilder for explicit runtime

Beads:
- Issue tracker metadata refresh

Cargo.lock:
- Dependency additions for asupersync in fsqlite-vdbe

Scripts:
- Add scripts/lint_no_os_threads.sh guardrail to detect raw
  std::thread::spawn in production engine crate sources

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1b5e","title":"5A.2: Implement sqlite_master row insert/delete for DDL operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:42:14.658309310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:34:56.002701373Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:34:56.002685002Z","close_reason":"5A.2 implemented: insert_sqlite_master_row + delete_sqlite_master_row + with_pager_write_txn + 5 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2yy6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-1b5e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# 5A.2: Implement sqlite_master row insert/delete for DDL operations

## What

Every DDL operation (CREATE TABLE, DROP TABLE, CREATE INDEX, DROP INDEX, CREATE VIEW, etc.)
must insert or delete a row in the sqlite_master table on page 1. This is how SQLite persists
schema — it's not metadata, it's actual B-tree data in a real table.

## sqlite_master schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE sqlite_master (
    type TEXT,      -- 'table', 'index', 'view', 'trigger'
    name TEXT,      -- object name
    tbl_name TEXT,  -- associated table (for indexes: parent table; for tables: same as name)
    rootpage INT,   -- B-tree root page number (0 for views/triggers)
    sql TEXT        -- original CREATE statement text (NULL for auto-indexes)
);
```

## Where to change

**Primary file:** `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`

Add methods:
- `fn insert_into_sqlite_master(&self, txn: &mut dyn TransactionHandle, type_: &str, name: &str, tbl_name: &str, rootpage: i32, sql: &str) -> Result<()>`
- `fn delete_from_sqlite_master(&self, txn: &mut dyn TransactionHandle, name: &str) -> Result<()>`

These methods open a BtCursor on page 1 (sqlite_master) and use the standard
record format to encode/decode rows.

## Record format for sqlite_master rows

Each row is a standard SQLite record:
```
[varint: header_size] [serial_type_for_type] [serial_type_for_name]
[serial_type_for_tbl_name] [serial_type_for_rootpage] [serial_type_for_sql]
[value_bytes...]
```

Where serial types are:
- TEXT columns: serial_type = 2*N + 13 where N = byte length
- INTEGER columns: serial_type depends on value magnitude (1-6 for various sizes)

## Implementation approach

1. Implement `fn encode_sqlite_master_record(type_: &str, name: &str, tbl_name: &str, rootpage: i32, sql: &str) -> Vec<u8>`
   using the existing record encoding in fsqlite-types (serial type + value encoding)
2. In execute_create_table():
   - Open BtCursor on page 1 (sqlite_master root)
   - Allocate next rowid for sqlite_master (track in Connection state or scan for max)
   - Call cursor.table_insert(rowid, encoded_record)
3. In execute_drop_table():
   - Open BtCursor on page 1
   - Scan for row where name matches
   - Call cursor.delete()
4. Increment schema cookie in header (offset 40) after each DDL operation

## Schema cookie

The schema cookie is a 32-bit counter at header offset 40. It increments on every DDL operation.
Prepared statements cache the cookie value; if it changes, they must re-prepare. This is how
SQLite detects schema changes.

## Considerations

- sqlite_master rowids are NOT the same as root pages — they're sequential 1, 2, 3...
- The sqlite_master table itself is always at page 1 with root page 1
- Auto-indexes (created implicitly for UNIQUE constraints) have sql=NULL
- The sql column stores the ORIGINAL CREATE statement text verbatim
- Temp tables go in sqlite_temp_master (separate concern, not this task)

## Tests

- `test_create_table_inserts_sqlite_master_row`: CREATE TABLE, verify row exists in page 1
- `test_drop_table_deletes_sqlite_master_row`: CREATE then DROP, verify row gone
- `test_schema_cookie_increments`: DDL operations increment cookie
- `test_sqlite_master_record_format`: Verify encoded record matches C SQLite format
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:44:38Z"},{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-1b5e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1b5e (5A.2) — sqlite_master row insert/delete for DDL

### Implementation Summary

Added three new methods to Connection in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:

1. with_pager_write_txn() — Auto-transaction helper that begins/commits/rollbacks a pager transaction when no explicit transaction is active.

2. insert_sqlite_master_row() — Serializes a 5-column record (type, name, tbl_name, rootpage, sql) using serialize_record, creates a BtCursor on page 1, and inserts with auto-incrementing rowid.

3. delete_sqlite_master_row() — Scans the page 1 B-tree for a row whose name column matches (case-insensitive), then calls cursor.delete().

### Wiring

- execute_create_table(): Both Columns and AsSelect branches now call insert_sqlite_master_row.
- execute_drop(): Table case calls delete_sqlite_master_row after removing from MemDatabase.

### Tests Added (5 total, all passing)

- test_create_table_inserts_sqlite_master_row
- test_create_multiple_tables_inserts_multiple_rows
- test_drop_table_deletes_sqlite_master_row
- test_create_drop_create_reinserts
- test_sqlite_master_record_format

### Quality Gates
- 3,875 tests passing (5 new), 1 pre-existing bead hygiene failure
- cargo check/clippy/fmt: all clean

Make the MVCC commit pipeline, intent log appending, and SSI read-set/write-set validation asynchronously yield when waiting for disk or locks.

### Implementation
1. Transition WriteCoordinator internal message passing to asupersync::channel::mpsc bounded channels.
2. Update concurrent_read_page and concurrent_write_page in MvccPager to .await disk/WAL reads when resolving historical tuple versions or write-write conflicts via the safe write-merge ladder.
3. Make SSI validation phase async so it can yield if it needs to swap in historical metadata from disk.

### Testing
- Unit: Verify WriteCoordinator channels respect backpressure (bounded capacity)
- Unit: SSI validation with simulated slow disk — verify yield and resume
- E2E: 10 concurrent writers with conflicting write sets — verify correct SSI abort behavior
- Logging: tracing::info! for transaction commit latency split into 'SSI Validation Time' and 'WAL Flush Wait Time'
","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:25:39Z"},{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-1b7j0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Enhanced Testing & Logging Spec (2026-03-07 review pass)

### Additional Unit Tests
- test_fcw_under_async: Two concurrent transactions modify same page → First-Committer-Wins: first to commit succeeds, second gets SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT → verify correctness under async yielding
- test_intent_log_async_append: Append 1000 intent log entries concurrently → verify ordering and no data loss (all entries present)
- test_ssi_read_set_validation_yield: SSI validation needs to read historical page version from disk → verify it yields, doesn't block scheduler, and completes correctly
- test_write_coordinator_channel_full: Fill WriteCoordinator's bounded channel → verify backpressure (sender blocks, doesn't drop messages)
- test_concurrent_mode_preserved: After async migration, verify concurrent_mode_default is still true and BEGIN promotes to BEGIN CONCURRENT

### Additional E2E Tests
- e2e_mvcc_consistency_under_async: 10 writers + 10 readers for 60 seconds → verify all reads see consistent snapshots (no phantom reads, no dirty reads)
- e2e_write_merge_ladder_async: Test the safe write merge ladder under async conditions — conflicting writes to different columns of same row should merge successfully

### Logging Requirements
- tracing::info!(target: "fsqlite::mvcc::commit", event = "commit_start", txn_id = %id, write_set_pages = %count)
- tracing::info!(target: "fsqlite::mvcc::commit", event = "commit_complete", txn_id = %id, ssi_validation_ms = %ssi_ms, wal_flush_ms = %wal_ms)
- tracing::warn!(target: "fsqlite::mvcc::commit", event = "conflict", txn_id = %id, conflicting_page = %pg, outcome = "abort")
- tracing::debug!(target: "fsqlite::mvcc::ssi", event = "read_set_check", txn_id = %id, pages_checked = %count, disk_reads = %disk)

### Acceptance Criteria
1. First-Committer-Wins behavior identical before and after async migration
2. concurrent_mode_default remains true (NEVER regressed)
3. No dropped messages in WriteCoordinator channels
4. SSI validation completes correctly even with slow disk (yields, doesn't deadlock)

-{"id":"bd-1b7j0.1","title":"Unify the production commit pipeline on asupersync reserve/send channels","description":"## Background & Why This Exists
The codebase already contains two different implementations of the same core idea: a cancel-safe, bounded, two-phase commit handoff.

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/commit_pipeline.rs` already wraps `asupersync::channel::mpsc` and exercises the native `reserve()/send()/abort()` semantics that Asupersync was chosen for.
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/commit_repair.rs` reimplements a separate two-phase queue in production using `Mutex`, `Condvar`, manual reservation sequencing, and custom permit types.

That split is strategically bad for three reasons:
1. Correctness logic is duplicated in two places, so the harness is no longer validating the exact primitive the engine uses on the real path.
2. Production is paying for a bespoke queue even though Asupersync already provides the exact cancel-safe handoff semantics we want.
3. Future async MVCC / coordinator work (`bd-1b7j0`, `bd-2jpu6.4`, `bd-2jpu6.5`) becomes harder if the queue semantics diverge across harness vs production.

This bead makes the production pipeline use the same native Asupersync channel model the harness already proves out, so reserve/send ordering, bounded backpressure, and cancellation behavior all have one source of truth.

## Scope
Focus on the production handoff path around `crates/fsqlite-core/src/commit_repair.rs` and any immediately-adjacent coordinator plumbing that currently depends on the custom queue types.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Replace the bespoke `TwoPhaseCommitSender` / `TwoPhaseCommitReceiver` / `SendPermit` queue in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/commit_repair.rs` with a production wrapper around `asupersync::channel::mpsc` reserve/send semantics.
2. Preserve the important invariants from the current implementation:
   - bounded capacity and visible occupancy,
   - FIFO by reservation order,
   - explicit `abort()` when a reserved slot is abandoned,
   - no silent data loss when cancellation happens between reserve and send.
3. Reuse the harness commit-pipeline semantics instead of inventing a second abstraction. If a thin FrankenSQLite-specific wrapper is still useful, it should be a shared adapter over the native Asupersync sender/receiver, not a second hand-rolled queue.
4. Thread `&Cx` or region-owned cancellation through the enqueue/dequeue path so a cancelled writer or poisoned database region unwinds via the same structured-concurrency rules as the rest of the migration program.
5. Ensure the migration composes with `WriteCoordinator` / MVCC commit ordering rather than changing FCW behavior or concurrent-writer defaults.

## Testing & Verification
- Unit: reserve one slot, cancel/abort before send, then verify capacity is returned immediately and no phantom commit is observable.
- Unit: have multiple writers reserve in order and send out-of-order; verify the exposed receive order still matches the intended reservation discipline.
- Unit: saturate the channel and prove bounded backpressure rather than message drops.
- Unit: inject cancellation between reserve and send and verify the receiver never observes a partially-abandoned request.
- E2E: concurrent writers under commit pressure still preserve FrankenSQLite's concurrent-writer default behavior and FCW conflict outcomes.
- Harness alignment: port or reuse the existing `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/commit_pipeline.rs` assertions so the harness and production queue semantics stay literally aligned.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Production no longer carries a separate Condvar-based two-phase commit queue for this path.
2. The real engine and the harness are using the same underlying Asupersync handoff semantics.
3. Cancellation between reserve and send is explicitly covered by deterministic tests.
4. No regression to concurrent-writer mode, FCW ordering, or bounded backpressure.

## Notes For Future Implementation
- This bead is intentionally about queue semantics and lifecycle integration, not a wholesale MVCC redesign.
- If a tracked-session or obligation-aware Asupersync channel variant is useful here, evaluate it, but do not expand scope into a speculative framework rewrite.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:06:58.042471498Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-08T01:06:58.042471498Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b7j0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7j0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1b7j0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jpu6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## What
Configure wasm-pack to produce a publishable npm package with TypeScript types, ES module output, and proper package.json.

## npm Package Structure
```
@frankensqlite/core/
├── package.json
├── frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm
├── frankensqlite_wasm.js      (ES module glue)
├── frankensqlite_wasm.d.ts    (TypeScript declarations)
```

## package.json
```json
{
  "name": "@frankensqlite/core",

  "description": "FrankenSQLite — concurrent-writer SQLite in the browser via WebAssembly",

  "files": ["frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm", "frankensqlite_wasm.js", "frankensqlite_wasm.d.ts"],
```

```yaml
# GitHub Actions step
- name: Build WASM

    wasm-opt -Os pkg/frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm -o pkg/frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm
```

- `wasm-pack build` produces valid npm package
- TypeScript types are correct and complete
- ES module import works: `import init, { FrankenDB } from '@frankensqlite/core'`

### Phase 6: MVCC Concurrent Writers with SSI
**Deliverables:** Transaction type (TxnId, Snapshot, write_set, intent_log, SSI state), snapshot capture, version chains (GF(256) delta encoding), page lock table (SharedPageLockTable SHM + InProcessPageLockTable), SSI witness plane (HotWitnessIndex + cold plane + witness objects), SSI validation (conservative pivot abort + refinement), conflict resolution (FCW + merge ladder: deterministic rebase + structured patch), GC (horizon, version trimming, witness GC), write coordinator (asupersync 2-phase MPSC), ARC cache (PageNumber,CommitSeq keys, MVCC-aware eviction), MvccPager trait.
**Acceptance:** Serialized mode = C SQLite behavior. Concurrent: disjoint pages both commit. Same page non-mergeable → SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT. 100 threads × 100 rows all present. Snapshot isolation (long reader, post-commit reader). Merge safety: structured pages MUST NOT XOR merge + B-tree lost-update counterexample. GC memory bounded. Version chain ≤ active txns + 1. Version chain compression >80% savings. SSI write skew abort. PRAGMA serializable=OFF allows both. Rebase merge distinct keys. Roaring bitmap exact. ARC adaptation (sequential scan ≠ evict hot index). Lab runtime 100 seeds. Mazurkiewicz 3-txn all orderings. E-process INV-1..7 zero violations. 2,000+ tests.
**Risk:** Hardest phase. Atomic snapshot capture. GC must not reclaim active versions. Merge ladder correctness. ARC+MVCC eviction complexity.
**Estimated:** ~15,000 LOC.

### Phase 7: Advanced Query Planner, Full VDBE, SQL Features
**Deliverables:** Full WHERE optimization (index scan, range, OR, LIKE prefix, skip-scan), join ordering (beam search, mxChoice), all 190+ VDBE opcodes, window functions (ROWS/RANGE/GROUPS, EXCLUDE), CTEs (materialized + recursive), triggers (BEFORE/AFTER/INSTEAD OF + recursive), foreign keys (deferred/immediate, CASCADE), view expansion, ALTER TABLE (RENAME/ADD/DROP COLUMN), VACUUM/VACUUM INTO, REINDEX, ANALYZE (sqlite_stat1).
**Acceptance:** Index selection via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN. Partial/expression indexes. 4-table join ordering. Window functions. Recursive CTE (Fibonacci). Triggers with validation/audit. Foreign key CASCADE. VACUUM INTO. 3,000+ tests.
**Risk:** WHERE optimizer (where.c ~7,800 LOC). Cost estimation heuristics must match C SQLite.
**Estimated:** ~20,000 LOC.

### Phase 8: Extensions
**Deliverables:** All §14 extensions in separate crates (JSON1, FTS5, FTS3/4, R*-Tree, Session, ICU, Misc).
**Acceptance per extension:** JSON1 JSONB round-trip + json_each/json_tree (200 tests). FTS5 100K docs + BM25 + highlight/snippet. FTS3/4 matchinfo format match. R*-Tree 100K 2D + custom geometry. Session changeset generate/apply. ICU locale collation. generate_series 1M < 1s.
**Dependencies:** Phase 7 (virtual table API).
**Estimated:** ~25,000 LOC.

### Phase 9: CLI, Conformance, Benchmarks, Replication
**Deliverables:** CLI (frankentui, dot-commands, output modes, tab completion, syntax highlighting, history). Conformance harness + golden file comparison. 1,000+ SQL test files. Criterion benchmarks. Fountain-coded replication (UDP + receiver + changeset). Snapshot shipping.
**Acceptance:** All sqlite3 dot-commands. **100% conformance parity** (intentional divergences documented). Single-writer within 3x C SQLite. Multi-writer linear scaling to 4 cores. Replication 10% loss within 1.2x no-loss. 4,000+ tests.
**Dependencies:** Phase 8.
**Estimated:** ~10,000 LOC.

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.1","title":"Rewrite fsqlite-vfs trait to be fully asynchronous","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
The `VfsFile` trait currently defines synchronous read/write methods. In `uring.rs`, `pollster::block_on` stalls the OS thread to wait for the `io_uring` completion queue. This destroys throughput for concurrent connections.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Refactor `VfsFile::read` and `VfsFile::write` to be `async` (`impl Future<Output = Result<usize>> + Send`).
2. Eradicate `pollster::block_on` from `crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/uring.rs`.
3. Update Unix/Memory VFS fallbacks. Use `asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking_io` for synchronous FDs so the main async executor isn't blocked.
4. Propagate the `&Cx` capability context through all await boundaries, checking cancellation status periodically.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Implement strict tests verifying the VFS successfully multiplexes 100 concurrent reads onto a single `io_uring` submission queue without thread blocking.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** Create `scripts/verify_async_vfs.sh`. It must launch a test binary that logs `tracing::trace` events. The script must parse the JSON logs and assert that multiple `read_at_start` events occur sequentially *before* their corresponding `read_at_complete` events, definitively proving multiplexing.

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.2","title":"Adapt B-Tree and Page Cache layers to support async reads/writes","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
The Pager manages caching and disk I/O. If multiple concurrent queries miss the cache for the same page, we must not issue redundant `io_uring` reads, and we cannot block thread execution while waiting for the disk.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Update `PageCache::read_page` and `write_page` to be `async fn`.
2. **I/O Coalescing (Flight Control):** Add an `InFlight` tracking map to `PageCache`. If Task B requests Page X while Task A is already reading it from VFS, Task B must await a `Notify` channel tied to Task A's completion rather than issuing a duplicate read.
3. **Async Eviction:** Ensure the eviction algorithm (e.g., S3-FIFO) does not synchronously block on writing dirty pages. Dirty evictions must be dispatched to a background `asupersync` Region task.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Mock the VFS with an artificial 50ms delay. Spawn 10 async tasks requesting the same uncached page. Assert that the VFS `read` method is called exactly once.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** Create `scripts/verify_pager_coalescing.sh`. Measure cache admits vs. VFS read syscalls under high concurrent contention and assert that coalescing prevents read amplification.

### From bd-v58jq.2 — Async Pager and TransactionHandle Traits:
1. **Asynchronous Page States:** Update PageCache to support a `Fetching` state using asupersync::sync::Notify or one-shot channels. Multiple queries requesting the same cache-missed page must multiplex onto a single disk fetch (I/O coalescing).
2. **S3-FIFO Concurrent Safety:** Update S3-FIFO and ARC eviction policies to handle concurrent async insertions cleanly without corrupting MRU/LRU state machines (using atomic operations or asupersync::sync::Mutex).
3. **Async Eviction:** Dirty evictions must be dispatched to a background asupersync Region task, not synchronously blocking.

### From bd-v58jq.3 — Async B-Tree Cursor Traversal:
1. Convert `load_page`, `advance_next`, `advance_prev`, `table_seek`, `index_seek` to async.
2. **Prevent Allocation Overhead:** CRITICAL — avoid boxing every future (Box::pin); use state machines or concrete future types to avoid heap thrashing on every B-Tree node traversal. Page stack (self.stack) must safely persist across await boundaries with no hidden RefCell or non-Send types.
3. Refactor `prefetch_page_hint` to actually spawn non-blocking concurrent fetch tasks into an asupersync region.

### Testing (merged)
- Unit: Mock VFS with 50ms delay, 10 async tasks requesting same uncached page → assert VFS read called exactly once (I/O coalescing)
- Unit: Verify page stack persists across yield points without memory leaks
- E2E: Full table scan with concurrent writers, measure page cache hit/miss ratio
- Logging: tracing::debug for cache miss latency, fetch coalescing events, cursor yield/resume

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.3","title":"Refactor VDBE execution loop to be async-aware","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
The `fsqlite-vdbe` bytecode interpreter currently executes instructions synchronously. To leverage the async Pager/B-Tree, the VDBE must become an async state machine that pipelines I/O requests to saturate the `io_uring`.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Convert `Vdbe::step()` into an `async fn`.
2. Opcodes like `SeekRowid`, `Next`, and `Column` must eagerly `.await` the new async B-Tree APIs.
3. **Vectorized Pipelining:** For operations operating on batches (e.g., full table scans), pre-fetch the next N pages asynchronously via `futures::future::join_all` while the CPU processes the current page.
4. Insert `asupersync::runtime::yield_now()` into long-running synchronous VDBE routines (like sorting large datasets) to maintain scheduler fairness.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Ensure the VDBE correctly preserves its program counter and register state across async yields.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** Create `scripts/verify_vdbe_pipelining.sh`. Run an analytical `SUM()` query over a 10GB table. Profile `io_uring` submission depth and assert that the VDBE successfully keeps the queue depth saturated (>16 concurrent inflight reads).

-{"id":"bd-2jpu6.4","title":"Replace OS thread spawning with asupersync structured Regions and Tasks","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
Currently, the engine uses raw `std::thread::spawn` for lifecycle tasks, GC, and IPC. This breaks `asupersync`'s structured concurrency, preventing deterministic shutdown and task tracking.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Search and eradicate all `std::thread::spawn` usage in `crates/` (except specific testing harnesses where unavoidable).
2. Refactor background systems to use `state.create_task(region_id, budget, async_block)`.
3. Create a master `EngineRootRegion`. All background GC and snapshot maintenance tasks must be children of this region.
4. Implement a graceful shutdown sequence that calls `cancel_region()` on the root and `.await`s the termination of all sub-tasks.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Create an engine, start background tasks, trigger shutdown, and assert that the `asupersync` executor's task count drops to zero.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** `scripts/lint_no_os_threads.sh`. A strict AST/grep linter that will fail CI if `std::thread::spawn` or `std::thread::Builder` are introduced into the core engine crates.

2. Use cx.checkpoint() and cx.yield_now() to prevent starvation of foreground queries.

+{"id":"bd-2jpu6.4","title":"Replace OS thread spawning with asupersync structured Regions and Tasks","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
Currently, the engine uses raw `std::thread::spawn` for lifecycle tasks, GC, and IPC. This breaks `asupersync`'s structured concurrency, preventing deterministic shutdown and task tracking.

## Implementation Tasks
1. Search and eradicate all `std::thread::spawn` usage in `crates/` (except specific testing harnesses where unavoidable).
2. Refactor background systems to use `state.create_task(region_id, budget, async_block)`.
3. Create a master `EngineRootRegion`. All background GC and snapshot maintenance tasks must be children of this region.
4. Implement a graceful shutdown sequence that calls `cancel_region()` on the root and `.await`s the termination of all sub-tasks.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Create an engine, start background tasks, trigger shutdown, and assert that the `asupersync` executor's task count drops to zero.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** `scripts/lint_no_os_threads.sh`. A strict AST/grep linter that will fail CI if `std::thread::spawn` or `std::thread::Builder` are introduced into the core engine crates.

1. Convert MVCC GC, Hot Witness Indexes, and Lock Table maintenance loops into `async fn(cx: &Cx) -> Result<()>`.
2. Use cx.checkpoint() and cx.yield_now() to prevent starvation of foreground queries.
3. If GC loop hits unrecoverable state, return Err to trigger database poisoning.
4. Expose tuning PRAGMAs: `PRAGMA mvcc_gc_interval_ms` via shared atomics.
- Tests: test_mvcc_gc_quiescence, test_mvcc_gc_dynamic_tuning_via_pragma

### From bd-3jzzb — WAL Background Workers:
1. Convert WAL checkpointing and FEC repair workers to async.
2. **Safe Drain Protocol (Multi-phase):** On cancellation, WAL worker catches cancel, enters finalize phase, completes pending fsync flushes.
3. Fatal I/O handling: fsync ENOSPC/EIO → immediately poison database state.
4. Expose PRAGMAs: `PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint`, `PRAGMA wal_flush_delay_ms`.
- Tests: test_wal_shared_graceful_shutdown, test_wal_fatal_io_triggers_poisoning

### From bd-1cs00.2 — Refactor background workers to asupersync::Region:
1. All background workers must be children of a per-database Region.
2. Master EngineRootRegion → Database Regions → Worker Tasks hierarchy.

### Observability:
- `tracing::debug!(target: "fsqlite_mvcc::region", ...)` for lifecycle events
- `tracing::info!` for checkpoint boundaries, page transfer counts, background I/O duration

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.5","title":"Ensure End-to-End Cancel Correctness via &Cx and Task Cancellation","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
Cancel-correctness is the ultimate test of an async architecture. If a user cancels a query (e.g., HTTP request timeout), the Future is dropped. If dropped mid-transaction, we must not leak memory, leave latches poisoned, or corrupt the B-Tree.

## Implementation Tasks
1. **Drop Guards:** Wrap active Transactions and VDBE states in custom `Drop` implementations. If dropped while active, the transaction MUST immediately trigger a rollback via the MVCC undo log.
2. **Cancellation Shielding:** Ensure that the strictly synchronous Write-Phase of B-Tree modifications (mandated in the OLC task) is shielded from cancellation. Once a page split begins modifying bytes in the PageCache, it must run to completion.
3. Validate that `&Cx` cancellation signals are checked precisely at safe yield boundaries (e.g., before beginning a new VDBE opcode).

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Manually `drop()` a VDBE `step()` Future halfway through an `Insert` opcode. Assert that the B-Tree remains traversable and the transaction rolls back cleanly.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** `scripts/verify_cancel_correctness.sh`. Use `fslab` EProcess oracles to mathematically prove safety. Inject 10,000 random Future drops during execution. The oracle must assert that the database file passes `PRAGMA integrity_check` every single time.

This task now additionally covers two-phase safety for B-Tree mutations:

### Two-Phase B-Tree Mutations (from bd-1cs00.3):
1. **WritePermit paradigm:** `reserve_write_page()` allocates and locks everything infallibly (phase 1). `.commit()` applies byte changes synchronously and atomically (phase 2).
2. This maps directly to asupersync's two-phase reserve/send pattern: reserve is the fallible, cancellable phase; commit is the infallible, shielded phase.
3. Intent log approach: before physical modification, log the intended changes. If cancelled between reserve and commit, the intent log enables clean rollback.
4. **Cancellation Shielding:** Once a page split begins modifying bytes in PageCache, it MUST run to completion. The Write-Phase is synchronous and uninterruptible.
5. **Drop Guards:** Active Transactions and VDBE states wrapped in custom Drop implementations. If dropped while active, transaction MUST immediately trigger rollback via MVCC undo log.

### Observability (from bd-1cs00):
- `tracing::trace!(target: "fsqlite_btree::mutation", ...)` for two-phase permit acquisitions

### Testing:
- Unit: Manually drop() a VDBE step() Future halfway through Insert opcode → assert B-Tree remains traversable and transaction rolls back
- Unit: Cancel during phase-1 reserve → verify no page corruption
- Unit: Verify commit() after reserve() is infallible under all conditions
- Property: proptest arbitrary cancel points during B-Tree splits → no corrupted nodes

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.5.1","title":"Close VDBE and B-Tree checkpoint blind spots on the real execution path","description":"## Background & Why This Exists
The current codebase already threads `&Cx` into several storage interfaces, but the actual long-running execution loops are inconsistent about observing it:

- VFS backends checkpoint at I/O edges.
- Tiered storage and RaptorQ integration perform periodic checkpoint checks.
- But the real VDBE interpreter loop still does not take `&Cx` on the hot path, and long B-Tree seek / iteration loops that already receive `&Cx` still have large blind spots with no `checkpoint()` calls.

That means the engine has cancellation-awareness at some outer edges while still leaving real CPU-bound traversal loops comparatively blind. This bead exists to close that gap explicitly instead of assuming the big async-migration beads will eventually absorb it.

It also creates the right foundation for the later Track 6 checkpoint-cost optimization work: first prove the correct poll sites and latency envelope, then optimize their tax. Do not skip directly to the fast-path work while the hot loops still have missing observation points.

## Scope
Focus on the real execution path, especially:
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`
- `crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs`
- any adjacent helper loops that run for many iterations without an I/O boundary

## Implementation Tasks
1. Inventory the hot loops on the real runtime path and mark where cancellation is currently observed vs silently deferred.
2. Plumb `&Cx` through the VDBE execution path where it is still missing, in a way that composes with the broader `bd-2jpu6.3` async-aware VDBE work.
3. Add bounded checkpoint cadences to long B-Tree seek / scan loops that already receive `&Cx`, using explicit safe points rather than ad hoc scattered checks.
4. Define and document the expected cancellation-latency budget for these loops so later optimization work has a concrete contract to preserve.
5. Emit enough tracing / counters to prove where checkpoint observations happen and how long cancellation takes to surface in representative scans/seeks.

## Testing & Verification
- Unit: cancel a long VDBE scan and verify it aborts within the stated instruction / latency bound.
- Unit: cancel long B-Tree seek / iteration loops and verify the traversal stops without corrupting cursor state.
- Unit: ensure newly-added checkpoint sites do not alter result correctness for normal execution.
- E2E: run long scans with induced cancellation and capture deterministic evidence for cancellation latency.
- Cross-track alignment: explicitly record how this bead feeds the later `bd-1dp9.6.7.12*` fast-path optimization work, so those beads optimize an already-correct poll surface instead of inventing a new one.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The real VDBE and B-Tree hot loops have explicit, tested checkpoint observation sites.

 {"id":"bd-2jpu6.6","title":"Establish synchronous FFI and WASM boundary adapters","description":"
## Background & Reasoning
We must preserve the engine's external compatibility. The C-API expects synchronous behavior, and standard Rust consumers may want to use a blocking `Connection` without setting up an async runtime. WASM targets lack full multi-threading support.

## Implementation Tasks
1. **CurrentThread Executor:** For the C-API (`fsqlite-c-api`), spin up a thread-local `asupersync::runtime::RuntimeBuilder::current_thread()` executor. When `sqlite3_step` is called, synchronously `block_on` the async VDBE step. This provides absolute zero-overhead execution without thread-hopping.
2. **Dual Rust API:** Update the `fsqlite` crate to provide `AsyncConnection` alongside the existing blocking `Connection` (which wraps the async API in a local executor).
3. **WASM Adapter:** In `fsqlite-wasm`, use a single-threaded WASM executor. Export an async JS API (Promises) mapped to Rust futures, and evaluate `Asyncify` ONLY if strict synchronous JS emulation is demanded by downstream legacy consumers.

## Testing & Verification
- **Unit Tests:** Execute the standard SQLite C-API conformance suite (`test_compat`) against the new adapter to prove 100% behavioral parity.
- **E2E / Diagnostic Script:** `scripts/verify_sync_adapters.sh`. Run an intense multi-connection workload through the C-API. Ensure the `CurrentThread` executor manages its own VFS interactions smoothly without cross-thread deadlocks.

This track makes sure the optimization program stays scientifically honest. +#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Guardrail for bd-2jpu6.4:
+# fail if production engine code reintroduces raw OS-thread spawning.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_PATH="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
+if [[ "${SCRIPT_PATH}" != /* ]]; then
+  SCRIPT_PATH="$(pwd)/${SCRIPT_PATH}"
+fi
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${SCRIPT_PATH}")/.." && pwd)"
+cd "${REPO_ROOT}"
+
+TARGET_DIRS=(
+  "crates/fsqlite/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-core/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-vfs/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-pager/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-wal/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-btree/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-observability/src"
+  "crates/fsqlite-cli/src"
+)
+
+FORBIDDEN_PATTERN='std::thread::spawn|thread::spawn|std::thread::Builder|thread::Builder'
+declare -a HITS=()
+
+scan_file() {
+  local file="$1"
+  local first_test_attr
+  local output
+  first_test_attr="$(
+    rg -n -m1 '^[[:space:]]*#\[(cfg|cfg_attr)\([^]]*\btest\b[^]]*\)\]' "${file}" \
+      | cut -d: -f1 \
+      || true
+  )"
+
+  if [[ -n "${first_test_attr}" ]]; then
+    local production_end=$((first_test_attr - 1))
+    if (( production_end <= 0 )); then
+      return
+    fi
+    output="$(
+      sed -n "1,${production_end}p" "${file}" \
+      | grep -nE "${FORBIDDEN_PATTERN}" \
+      | sed "s|^|${file}:|" \
+      || true
+    )"
+  else
+    output="$(rg -n "${FORBIDDEN_PATTERN}" "${file}" || true)"
+  fi
+
+  if [[ -n "${output}" ]]; then
+    while IFS= read -r line; do
+      HITS+=("${line}")
+    done <<< "${output}"
+  fi
+}
+
+while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
+  scan_file "${file}"
+done < <(
+  find "${TARGET_DIRS[@]}" \
+    -type f \
+    -name '*.rs' \
+    ! -name '*_tests.rs' \
+    ! -path '*/src/bin/*' \
+    -print0
+)
+
+if (( ${#HITS[@]} > 0 )); then
+  echo "[FAIL] raw OS-thread usage detected in production engine code:" >&2
+  printf '%s\n' "${HITS[@]}" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "[PASS] no raw OS-thread spawn/builders found in production engine crate sources"
